step_by_step_thinking:
The claim that the distal palatal termination of the maxillary complete denture base should be at the fovea palatine appears to be well-supported by evidence. This landmark serves as an effective boundary because it separates hard from soft tissue on the palate—providing both functional and physiological stability for the denture. Extending beyond this area may lead to discomfort due to irritation of sensitive mucosal surfaces, especially during swallowing and speech. Therefore, anchoring the base here offers optimal comfort and function without compromising oral health outcomes.

Final decision: I agree with option B — the fovea palatine.
